text: The surgeon digitally evaluates the perineal area, assessing for signs of weakness.
The surgeon performs a bilateral perineal incision at the ischial tuberosity, revealing underlying tissue structures.
The surgeon uses a blunt trochar to dissect anteriorly towards the transverse perineal muscle, then passes an O-proline suture through the dissected area.
The surgeon places a previously tailored biological mesh into position using a proline suture.
The surgeon secures the biological mesh to the aponeurotic bony structures using two O-PDS sutures, ensuring stable fixation within the surgical site.
The surgeon closes the skin using trio-vicryl rapid interrupted sutures, while digitally ensuring the resistance and strength of the perineal area.
